Docker Compose Development Configuration: The docker-compose.yml defines a local development stack with six services: the API server (Python/FastAPI), a PostgreSQL 15 database, a Redis 7 cache, an Nginx reverse proxy, a Celery worker, and a Flower monitoring dashboard. All service images are built from local Dockerfiles with hot-reload volumes mounted so code changes reflect without container restarts. Environment variables are sourced from a .env file that is gitignored; a .env.example with placeholder values is provided for new developer setup. Port mappings expose the API on 8000, PostgreSQL on 5432 (localhost only), and Flower on 5555.
